extra.reasoning: The property G(opted_out ⇒ not sent) holds on all reachable executions because the invariant ¬(opted_out ∧ sent) is inductive. Initial state has opted_out=false, so it holds. Each transition preserves it: t1 (optout) sets opted_out:=true but simultaneously sets sent:=false, so the conjunction is false; t2 (send_attempt) sets sent:=true only under guard 'not opted_out', so opted_out is false in the resulting state; t3 (send_attempt when opted_out) sets sent:=false; t4 (tick) sets sent:=false. The only transition that makes sent true is t2, which is gated on opted_out being false, and the only transition that makes opted_out true is t1, which always clears sent. Therefore no reachable state (within 8 events or otherwise) can have both opted_out and sent true.
